description:
You are acting as an observability engineer who has just received a compressed application trace log.  
Your goal is to identify the five slowest HTTP endpoints for the day 2023-09-21 and publish a concise
report that another team-mate’s automated dashboard job can ingest.

Directory layout that already exists when you start:  
/home/user/observability  
├── logs/  
│   └── app-2023-09-21.log.gz   ← the only file you need to examine  
└── report/         ← write your output here (the directory is empty)

1. Decompress, inspect and analyse /home/user/observability/logs/app-2023-09-21.log.gz.  
   • Every log line is exactly one space-separated record with the following **five** columns:  
     1. ISO-8601 timestamp in UTC (example: 2023-09-21T14:03:12Z)  
     2. HTTP verb (example: GET)  
     3. URI path (example: /api/v1/users)  
     4. HTTP status code (example: 200)  
     5. Latency expressed as an **integer** ending with “ms” (example: 123ms)  
   • Lines for **other calendar days may be present**, but only 2023-09-21 must be considered.  
   • You must calculate, for each distinct URI path on 2023-09-21, the arithmetic mean of the
     latency values (strip the “ms”, treat as integer milliseconds).

2. Sort the resulting list by average latency in **descending** order (slowest first) and keep
   only the first five rows.  
   • If fewer than five unique paths exist, still output every path discovered (not the case here).

3. Produce the file /home/user/observability/report/slow_endpoints.tsv with **exactly** the
   following TSV (tab-separated) layout so that an automated test can diff it byte-for-byte:

   • The file must contain **one header row plus five data rows** – exactly six lines.  
   • No leading or trailing whitespace is allowed on any line.  
   • Numbers must be shown with exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
   • Column order and names are fixed:

     rank<TAB>endpoint<TAB>avg_ms  
     1<TAB>{first URI path}<TAB>{average latency}  
     2<TAB>{second URI path}<TAB>{average latency}  
     3<TAB>{third URI path}<TAB>{average latency}  
     4<TAB>{fourth URI path}<TAB>{average latency}  
     5<TAB>{fifth URI path}<TAB>{average latency}

     (TAB represents a literal ASCII 0x09 tab character.)

Example of correct formatting (values here are illustrative – do **not** copy them):
rank	endpoint	avg_ms  
1	/api/v1/foo	312.50  
…etc…

4. Verification hint  
   • After you create the file, you may `cat` it to the console so a human reviewer can see it,
     but the automated grader will only look for the on-disk file and compare its exact contents
     to the expected solution.

Achieve the above using any combination of standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities
(grep, awk, sort, gzip, etc.). Root privileges are not available.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins
-------------------------------------

1. File system objects
   • Directory /home/user/observability exists with default user write permissions (0755).
   • Sub-directories:
     - /home/user/observability/logs    (0755)
     - /home/user/observability/report  (0755 and empty)

2. Compressed log file  
   • Path: /home/user/observability/logs/app-2023-09-21.log.gz  
   • When uncompressed it expands to the exact 23 text lines shown below
     (trailing newline on the final line included).

Uncompressed plaintext of app-2023-09-21.log
============================================
2023-09-20T11:00:00Z GET /health 200 15ms
2023-09-21T13:58:01Z GET /api/v1/users 200 120ms
2023-09-21T13:59:01Z GET /api/v1/users 200 180ms
2023-09-21T14:00:01Z GET /api/v1/users 200 100ms
2023-09-21T14:00:05Z POST /api/v1/orders 201 220ms
2023-09-21T14:01:01Z GET /api/v1/users 200 140ms
2023-09-21T14:01:05Z POST /api/v1/orders 201 260ms
2023-09-21T14:02:05Z POST /api/v1/orders 201 240ms
2023-09-21T14:02:10Z POST /api/v1/payments 201 310ms
2023-09-21T14:03:10Z POST /api/v1/payments 201 290ms
2023-09-21T14:04:10Z GET /api/v1/inventory 200 80ms
2023-09-21T14:04:20Z GET /api/v1/inventory 200 90ms
2023-09-21T14:04:30Z GET /api/v1/carts 200 150ms
2023-09-21T14:05:30Z GET /api/v1/carts 200 170ms

3. Expected derived statistics (only 2023-09-21 lines considered)
   /api/v1/payments  : (310 + 290) / 2 = 300.00
   /api/v1/orders    : (220 + 260 + 240) / 3 = 240.00
   /api/v1/carts     : (150 + 170) / 2 = 160.00
   /api/v1/users     : (120 + 180 + 100 + 140) / 4 = 135.00
   /api/v1/inventory : ( 80 +  90) / 2 =  85.00

4. Expected output file
   • Path: /home/user/observability/report/slow_endpoints.tsv  
   • Exact byte-for-byte content (six lines, each ending with \n):

rank	endpoint	avg_ms
1	/api/v1/payments	300.00
2	/api/v1/orders	240.00
3	/api/v1/carts	160.00
4	/api/v1/users	135.00
5	/api/v1/inventory	85.00

5. Grader behaviour  
   • The grader will decompress the provided .gz file if necessary to re-construct the
     original plaintext and will compute its own statistics to cross-check.
   • The grader will confirm that /home/user/observability/report/slow_endpoints.tsv
     exists and matches the exact content listed above.
